chunk_summary: This section outlines practical 24-hour feeding plans for children with chronic cardiorespiratory disease, emphasizing daytime bolus feeding and nocturnal continuous enteral support to optimize calorie delivery and reduce fatigue.
learning_objectives: Understand the structure of 24-hour feeding plans for children with chronic cardiorespiratory disease.
Learn strategies for balancing oral feeding and enteral support to preserve feeding skills and ensure adequate calorie intake.
Identify calorie-dense complementary feeding options suitable for different age groups.
Adapt feeding plans during intercurrent illness to minimize fatigue and vomiting.
key_facts: Daytime feeding involves 5–6 bolus feeds, combining oral feeding and NG supplementation.
Nocturnal continuous enteral feeding delivers 30–50% of daily calories over 8–10 hours.
Complementary feeding should focus on calorie-dense semisolids like enriched khichdi, curd rice with oil, or dalia/kheer with powdered nuts.
Fortified expressed breast milk or concentrated formula is recommended for density optimization.
During illness, shift calories to smaller, frequent feeds and resume baseline plans during recovery.
reference_quote: ESPEN–ESPGHAN–ECFS guideline on nutrition care for cystic fibrosis (2024): Overnight feeds may provide up to about half of daily requirements when gastrostomy feeding is used.
